package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for repeated_string_trim_normalize: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: chains like `strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(strings.TrimPrefix(s, ...)))` that scan the string multiple times
type CaseRepeatedStringTrimNormalizeDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseRepeatedStringTrimNormalizeModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseRepeatedStringTrimNormalizeRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseRepeatedStringTrimNormalizeModel
}

type CaseRepeatedStringTrimNormalizeService struct {
    repo *CaseRepeatedStringTrimNormalizeRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseRepeatedStringTrimNormalizeAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeRepeatedStringTrimNormalize(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseRepeatedStringTrimNormalizeDTO) error {
    focus := "repeated_string_trim_normalize"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    normalized := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = normalized
    repeated_string_trim_normalize_negative := map[string]int{"repeated": 1, "string": 2, "trim": 3, "normalize": 4}
    repeated_string_trim_normalize_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    repeated_negative_0 := repeated_string_trim_normalize_negative["repeated"] + len(input)
    _ = repeated_negative_0
    string_negative_1 := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = string_negative_1
    trim_negative_2 := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = trim_negative_2
    normalize_negative_3 := repeated_string_trim_normalize_negative["normalize"] + len(input)
    _ = normalize_negative_3
    return nil
}
